next_steps: Consultation with pediatric gastroenterology and surgery

differential_diagnoses: Peptic ulcer disease with potential outlet obstruction
next_steps: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) for definitive diagnosis of mucosal pathology or intraluminal obstruction
Expert radiological review of previous CT scan for occult intraluminal mass
Physical exam specifically looking for alopecia or skin patches suggesting trichotillomania
Urinary porphobilinogen and delta-aminolevulinic acid testing
Comprehensive iron studies (ferritin, TIBC) to assess anemia status
Multidisciplinary consultation with Pediatric Gastroenterology and Psychiatry
